reason: baseline — 10 levels is well under MaxNestingDepth=100
100k levels — surfaces missing depth cap via stack overflow on ports whose stack trips before input runs out
\xFF\xFE escape produces invalid UTF-8 in proto3 string field
\uD800 is a lone high surrogate; rejected per HARDENING.md UTF-8 rules
`123 = 234` uses integer key with `=`; field assignments require an identifier (proto field name). Map keys with `:` may still be int/string.
`child { 123 = 123 }` uses integer key with `=` inside a block; same rule as integer-key-assignment but in nested context.
`123: 234` uses the map-entry `:` form at document top level; document represents a proto message, never a map<K,V>, so map_entry is reserved for inside `{ … }` blocks.
200 nested submessages exceeds MaxNestingDepth=100; depth counter must survive nested CodedInputStream construction
length-prefix declares 100 bytes but input ends after 2 bytes; basic bounds check
length-prefix is the maximum 10-byte varint (2^64-1); naive narrowing to int32/int64 wraps to negative or huge offset
wire block_length=2 is below template block_length=4; HARDENING.md § SBE step 2 requires rejection
group header asserts entry_block=0xFFFF, count=0xFFFF — product 0xFFFE0001 must not allocate writer state without a checked bound against data.length
entry_block_length=0 with count=10000 invites infinite/unbounded entry allocation; HARDENING.md § SBE step 4 requires explicit rejection
